name: Bud Powell
abstract: Earl Rudolph "Bud" Powell (September 27, 1924 – July 31, 1966) was an American jazz pianist and composer. A pioneer in the development of bebop and its associated contributions to jazz theory, Powell's application of complex phrasing to the piano influenced both his contemporaries and later pianists including Walter Davis Jr., Toshiko Ak…

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Mingus said, “C’mon Elvin” ... he says, “C’mon Elvin we’re going to play with Bud Powell.” ... It lasted a few months. Because Bud wasn’t all that consistent. So we played Cleveland and went to play in Detroit
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I thought it was tragic how many of our great musicians like Bird, like Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and so many others man, you know Bud Powell, that had tragic life circumstances
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: He had a big, huge collection and so he was sitting me down with Art Tatum and Oscar Peterson, Bill Evans and Bud Powell... from an early age.
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Earl May
about_quotes.evidence: I think what that comes from is playing with Billy and Bud Powell
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl May interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Broadbent
about_quotes.evidence: Bud for me and for a lot of like-minded people, Bud is the one where it was no longer ornamental music... This was a speaking voice, like a horn... Those are the things that are precious to me... what Bud in my own little reflective life I’d like to do something to somebody else... The Time is Now t
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Broadbent interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 26, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Bird allowed me to sit in. We played Dance of the Infidels. ... [INTERVIEWER] Bud Powell. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, which is normally played ... Bird said he played it ... but I was able to keep up with him ... as we played together
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
